category_title: Students with mild intellectual disabilities exhibiting academic engagement, off-task, or disruptive problem behaviors
minimal_context: The student is expected to listen to the teacher and answer questions during a general classroom lesson while seated near peers.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are going to go over the lesson now. Please sit at your desk, listen carefully, and be ready to answer questions.

STUDENT: (Looks outside through the window and starts playing with a pencil instead of listening.)

TUTOR: Please pay attention to the lesson.

STUDENT: (Turns around to the peer sitting at the desk behind them) Look at what I can do with my pencil.

TUTOR: Stop talking to your friends. You need to sit quietly and listen to me right now.

STUDENT: (Stands up without permission, walks over to the friend's desk, and grabs their eraser while talking aloud) I am taking this!

options.A: Approve the student's behaviors during the general classroom lesson.
options.B: Use music for relaxation or as a way to create meaningful break activities.
options.C: Stop the movement of the student's hand and lead them back to answering questions.
options.D: Use poems aimed at promoting reflection and reinforcement of mindfulness concepts.
